novelty: Session 6 summary: age-sex crossovers, polypharmacy shifts, drug clustering, temporal+geographic patterns

Top 100 Drugs Atlas.finding: Top 100 drugs cover 38.1% of signals; 43 F-dominated, 11 M-dominated, 46 balanced
Top 100 Drugs Atlas.significance: Ranitidine 93.5%F most biased, Docetaxel 11.2%F most male-biased in top 100

Sex-Reversal Drug Pairs.finding: 2,564 AEs (50.6%) show BOTH F and M signals. Top spread: Adverse drug reaction 9.65 LR spread
Sex-Reversal Drug Pairs.significance: Half of all AEs can flip sex-bias direction depending on the drug

Testosterone Intra-Drug Reversal.finding: Testosterone: prostatic disorder LR=8.87 (F) but CVA LR=-2.41 (M). Spread=11.28
Testosterone Intra-Drug Reversal.significance: Same drug, opposite sex-bias depending on organ system

Age-Sex Crossover - Liraglutide.finding: Liraglutide: 84%F in young adults → 27.7%F in elderly (-56.3pp shift)
Age-Sex Crossover - Liraglutide.significance: Most dramatic age-sex crossover: direction REVERSES between young and elderly

Age-Sex Crossover - Tirzepatide.significance: GLP-1 RA sex-bias is AGE-DEPENDENT, not intrinsic

Global Age-Sex Pattern.finding: Young adults 78%F → Elderly 59%F across all FAERS
Global Age-Sex Pattern.significance: Female reporting predominance narrows dramatically with age

Polypharmacy Sex-Bias Shift.finding: Combination drugs shift 17.2pp on average vs components. Aspirin+caffeine+paracetamol: 70%F→10%F (-60pp)
Polypharmacy Sex-Bias Shift.significance: Drug combinations can dramatically alter sex-differential safety profile

Drug Embedding Clusters.finding: 8 distinct drug clusters by signal profiles. Cluster 3 (opioids+APs) 78%F, Cluster 2 (respiratory) 35%F
Drug Embedding Clusters.significance: Drug safety signal profiles cluster by sex-bias, confirming pharmacological basis
